name: Serra Calderona Climbs Loop
name_original: Subida al puerto de Chirivilla — Circular desde Lliria
description: This difficult 52.8-mile road cycling route from Lliria climbs to the mythical Chirivilla Pass with 4515 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling around Llíria offers diverse landscapes, from the mountainous foothills of the Sierra Calderona range to agricultural areas along the River Turia. The region provides varied terrain suitable for different fitness levels, including challenging ascents and more leisurely rides. Cyclists can explore routes that traverse traditional Valencian landscapes, including orange groves and agricultural fields, often bordering the banks of the Túria River. This area is recognized for its extensive network of road cycling routes and natural features. Best road cycling routes around Llíria The most popular road cycling route is Serra Calderona Climbs Loop, a 52.8 miles (84.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain within the Sierra Calderona foothills. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Oronet Pass – Oronet Pass loop from Llíria, a difficult 46.2 miles (74.4 km) path. This route includes the notable Oronet Pass, offering sustained climbs and scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the Alcublas Village – Ojú Village loop from Llíria, a 48.2 miles (77.6 km) trail leading through rural villages, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around Llíria is defined by mountainous foothills, agricultural fields, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Llíria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 18 reviews. More than 2,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Llíria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Llíria?
A: Road cycling routes in Llíria offer a diverse landscape. You'll find mountainous foothills, particularly towards the Sierra Calderona range, providing varied terrain and scenic climbs. There are also extensive agricultural areas along the River Turia, featuring traditional Valencian landscapes like orange groves and fields. The region includes river valleys and rocky areas with towering cliffs, adding to the scenic variety.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Llíria?
A: Yes, Llíria offers routes suitable for all fitness levels. While many routes feature significant elevation, there are at least two easy routes available. For a very gentle option, the &lt;strong&gt;Llíria Greenway&lt;/strong&gt; is an adapted old railway line, offering a flat 5.73 km ride through agricultural landscapes and along the Túria River, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in Llíria?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Llíria provides several difficult routes, with nine such options available. These often involve significant elevation gain in the mountainous foothills of the Sierra Calderona. Notable challenging routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e843182221/serra-calderona-climbs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Calderona Climbs Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 84.9 km with over 1,300 meters of elevation, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32579881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oronet Pass – Oronet Pass loop from Llíria&lt;/a&gt;, known for its sustained climbs.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I visit while road cycling in Llíria?
A: Llíria's rich history allows cyclists to incorporate visits to ancient sites. You can explore the remains of an Iberian settlement on Cerro de San Miguel, Roman mausoleums and baths (Mura), or the 14th-century Neoclassic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6410512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Sant Miquel&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features several castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3149525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Real&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7894114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Benisanó&lt;/a&gt;, which can be interesting stops along your ride.

Q: Are there any natural parks or scenic areas to explore by bike in Llíria?
A: Absolutely. Llíria offers access to the extensive &lt;strong&gt;Turia River Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, with a 25 km main route along both banks of the river, ideal for cycling. The foothills of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Calderona&lt;/strong&gt; provide a mountainous backdrop with varied terrain. Just three kilometers from Llíria's center, &lt;strong&gt;San Vicente Park&lt;/strong&gt; is a 'green lung' with natural springs and trails. The rural area of &lt;strong&gt;La Concordia&lt;/strong&gt; also boasts considerable biodiversity with Mediterranean vegetation and wildlife.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Llíria?
A: The spring and autumn months are considered ideal for road cycling in Llíria, particularly April and October. During these periods, the weather is typically pleasant, offering comfortable temperatures for longer rides and challenging ascents without the extreme heat of summer.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes starting from Llíria?
A: Yes, Llíria serves as a starting point for several longer excursions. For instance, the Llíria-Alcublas-Oset-Llíria route spans 59.8 km with 710m elevation, while the El Remedio desde Llíria route is a significant 124.7 km with 1,337m elevation. These routes provide excellent opportunities for long-distance training or extended day trips.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Llíria?
A: The road cycling routes in Llíria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 18 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, which range from challenging mountainous climbs to scenic rides through agricultural fields and along river valleys. The variety of routes catering to different fitness levels is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there loop road cycling routes available in Llíria?
A: Many of the popular road cycling routes around Llíria are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32390667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olocau – Chirivilla Pass loop from Llíria&lt;/a&gt; (86.6 km) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32616394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcublas Village – Ojú Village loop from Llíria&lt;/a&gt; (77.6 km), which takes you through rural villages.

Q: Can I find family-friendly cycling options in Llíria?
A: While many road cycling routes are geared towards more experienced riders, the &lt;strong&gt;Llíria Greenway&lt;/strong&gt; offers a very family-friendly option. This easy 5.73 km route, built on an old railway line, is flat and safe, traversing traditional Valencian landscapes. It includes rest areas with shade and bicycle parking, making it suitable for families with children or those looking for a leisurely ride.

Q: Are there any caves I can explore near the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region around Llíria features several caves that can be explored. Notable examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5352202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Inés Cave&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2725733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Sacañé&lt;/a&gt;. These natural formations offer an interesting diversion and a glimpse into the geological features of the area, often accessible via short detours from cycling paths.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Llíria?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes available around Llíria, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. This extensive network includes routes from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every road cyclist.
tips: One of the mythical ports of the Sierra Calderona. Very quiet road that fills up with cyclists on the weekend, getting to experience an authentic cycling march. Essential!

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for the 'Climb to the port of Chirivilla — Circular from Lliria'?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a 'Hard road ride' and demands very good fitness from cyclists. Its significant length of nearly 85 kilometers and over 1,370 meters of elevation gain contribute to its high difficulty rating.

faqs.question: What is the road surface quality like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is generally well-paved and suitable for road bikes, with most sections being fully asphalted. However, be prepared for some segments that might be slightly bumpy or have loose gravel. Traffic is typically light along the climb.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the 'Climb to the port of Chirivilla — Circular from Lliria'?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Lliria, you can find parking within the town. Specific parking areas for cyclists are not detailed, but general public parking should be available in Lliria to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Are there public transport options to reach Lliria with a bike?
faqs.answer: Yes, Lliria is accessible by public transport from Valencia. You can typically use local train services (Metrovalencia Line 1) or bus routes that allow bikes, though it's always best to check specific operator policies and schedules for carrying bicycles before your trip.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Thanks to the mild Mediterranean climate, this climb is enjoyable throughout the year. However, spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer or cooler winter days.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Parc Natural de la Serra Calderona?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required for cycling on established routes within the Parc Natural de la Serra Calderona. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Is the 'Climb to the port of Chirivilla — Circular from Lliria' dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling, and while dogs are generally allowed in the natural park, bringing a dog along for a nearly 85 km road bike ride is not practical or recommended. It's best suited for human cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque views throughout the ride. The route winds through the Sierra Calderona, known for its abundant olive, orange, and almond trees. You'll pass through quiet rural villages like Gatova and Marines, and enjoy excellent views of the Calderona mountains, especially on the final 4 kilometers of the climb to the Chirivilla Pass.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other notable cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route partially overlaps with several other trails. You'll find sections that are part of [CIMA VA09] Pico del Águila (Chirivilla), [CIMA VA07] La Bellida, and [CIMA CS18] Montemayor. It also briefly touches upon longer routes like El Camí IT5TR7 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana and De Valencia a Lisboa.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through rural villages like Gatova and Marines, specific cafes or refreshment stops are not guaranteed at all times. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks. Lliria, as your starting and ending point, offers various options for food and drink.

faqs.question: What makes the Port of Chirivilla a significant cycling destination?
faqs.answer: The Port of Chirivilla is recognized as a 'mythical port' within the Sierra Calderona. On weekends, the road becomes lively with cyclists, creating an 'authentic cycling march' atmosphere. Its strategic location also allows cyclists to cross between the provinces of Castellón and Valencia.
